headline: IRAN DEAL LIFTS GOLD: Metal Snaps Two-Day Slide to Trade Near $4,750
snippet: Gold reversed a two-day decline on April 22-23 after an extended US-Iran ceasefire renewed safe-haven buying, with spot prices recovering toward $4,750/oz — still well below January's all-time high of $5,589 but gaining on geopolitical support.

headline: GHANA CRACKS DOWN: Newmont, AngloGold, Zijin Get December Deadline to Localize Mining Ops
snippet: Ghana's Minerals Commission issued a December 2026 ultimatum to Newmont, AngloGold Ashanti, and Zijin to hand all surface and underground mining operations over to Ghanaian-owned contractors, threatening sanctions for non-compliance with local content rules revised in January 2025.

headline: COPPER BULL DIVIDE: Goldman Holds $12,650 as Traxys Eyes $15,000 on Supply Squeeze
snippet: Goldman Sachs is maintaining its 2026 copper price forecast at $12,650/t while commodity trader Traxys projects a surge to $15,000/t — an analyst split driven by divergent views on how hard a potential 15% US tariff on refined copper will squeeze global supply chains.

headline: Saudi Mining Giant Quietly Abandons Global Acquisition Strategy
snippet: Saudi Arabia's state mining investment vehicle is stepping back from its aggressive global M&A push, a significant pivot that removes one of the sector's most deep-pocketed sovereign buyers from the deal market and reshapes junior mining takeover expectations.

headline: NINE DEAD: Gunmen Attack Copper-Gold Mine in Pakistan's Balochistan
snippet: At least nine people were killed when armed gunmen stormed National Resources Ltd's copper and gold project in Pakistan's Balochistan province on Wednesday, the latest in a string of security incidents targeting resource projects in the region.

headline: RARE EARTH SURGE: USA Rare Earth Climbs Again as $2.8B Brazil Deal Counters China Grip
snippet: USA Rare Earth (USAR) gained again on April 22 as markets continued pricing in the strategic value of its $2.8B Serra Verde acquisition — whose Pela Ema mine in Brazil is the only operation outside Asia capable of supplying all four magnetic rare earths at commercial scale.

headline: Freeport-McMoRan Crosses $100B Market Cap — Only 7th Miner in History to Do So
snippet: Freeport-McMoRan surged 4% to become only the seventh mining company in history to hit a $100B-plus market cap, as the copper major prepares to report Q1 earnings with its Indonesian mine operations approaching full production.

headline: SILVER LAGGING GOLD: At $78 It's Still 35% Below January Record as Deficit Widens
snippet: Silver spot at $78.87 remains 35% below its January 29 record of $121.64, even as analysts point to a projected 67-million-ounce supply deficit in 2026 and a weakening US dollar as the likely catalysts for the metal's next move higher.

headline: TSX-V DRILL HIT: Ramp Metals Returns 21m at 0.80% Cu, 17 g/t Ag at Rottenstone SW
snippet: Ramp Metals (TSX-V: RAMP) reported its first winter drill hole at Rottenstone SW in Saskatchewan cut 21m grading 0.80% Cu, 17.01 g/t Ag, 0.09 g/t Au and 0.73% Zn from 50.5m depth, with VMS-style mineralization confirmed in every hole drilled along the 1,100m Rush anomaly.

headline: SUPPLY SHOCK WARNING: Mercuria Says Aluminum Faces 'Black Swan' Disruption
snippet: Commodity trader Mercuria is warning that aluminum could face a 'black swan' supply shock — a development that would ripple through battery metal supply chains, smelting-intensive mining projects, and any junior targeting aluminum-linked critical minerals.

headline: Contango Silver & Gold Outlines $63M Exploration Budget Across Alaska-BC Critical Metals Portfolio
snippet: Contango Silver & Gold Inc. unveiled a $63 million 2026 exploration budget spanning advanced gold and critical metals projects in Alaska and Northern British Columbia, one of the larger junior exploration spending commitments announced so far this year.
